what_it_does: Renders a fixed 1600x900 static composition titled "Small Pieces Carry Whole Stories" with a narrative ledger-style grid of numbers, chips, claim/proven bullets, named roles, and the coined term "the Almanac".
observations: Uses Google Fonts plus an external `/surface/styles/vars.css` token sheet for colors, spacing, radius, and type sizes.
Centers a `.view` canvas on the page with a radial background gradient and a faint blueprint grid in the top-left corner.
Lays out five content cells in a 3-column by 2-row CSS grid, with the left "The Figure Argues" cell spanning both rows.
The largest visual element is the gold "365" figure with supporting subfigures "9", "1:1", and "12h".
Includes purely CSS-drawn markers: triangular bullets, checkbox ticks, empty circular avatars, a bottom hatch wash, and a circular "V" mark.
Contains no JavaScript, form controls, links in the body, or dynamic behavior.
standouts: The file frames small factual units as story-bearing atoms: figures, chips, claims, people, and a named ledger.
The "Said and Done" section contrasts claimed work with completed work using separate visual symbols.
The coined term "the Almanac" is given typographic emphasis and explanatory body copy.
conventions: Self-contained HTML document with embedded page-specific CSS.
Uses short class names and token-driven CSS variables rather than hard-coded local color and spacing values in most places.
Uses fixed viewport dimensions rather than responsive layout rules.
Uses decorative CSS pseudo-elements and gradients instead of image assets.
concerns: The composition is locked to 1600x900 and may not adapt to smaller screens.
Several color, spacing, and type values depend on external CSS variables, so the file is incomplete without `/surface/styles/vars.css`.
Avatar circles are placeholders with no identifying visual content.
notes: A static design-board page presenting narrative atoms as ledger entries, figures, roles, and proof markers.
questions: Is the "V" mark a version marker, brand mark, or section marker?
Are the empty avatar circles intentional placeholders or meant to receive portraits or initials?
Should the 1600x900 canvas be treated as a slide/artboard target rather than a responsive web page?
purpose_vs_actual: The title and content indicate a page about smallest parts carrying stories, and the actual file presents a visual inventory of small narrative/design atoms.
intention_signals: It seems intended to establish a visual language where numbers, roles, checked actions, and coined names function as compact story substrates inside a composed design system.
novelty: The file treats basic UI atoms such as numbers, chips, bullets, avatars, and labels as narrative artifacts rather than generic interface components.
contribution: Provides a fixed artboard composition for expressing ledger-like narrative atoms: emphasized figures, tactile chips, claim/proof pairs, human roles, coined terminology, blueprint texture, and warm craft-oriented token styling.
summary_for_embedding: Static 1600x900 HTML/CSS artboard showing a token-styled grid of narrative atoms including a large 365 figure, chips, claim/proof bullets, role rows, and an Almanac text block.
intention_for_embedding: The file appears meant to demonstrate how small code/design substrates can carry story through measured figures, evidence markers, named hands, and ledger language.
